1.premise: Conspiracy claims can become true through literal reframing.

lines.text: - I'm a huge fan of Alex Jones.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I like him a lot. I'm a big fan of Alex Jones.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: The thing about him is he says some things that are true, right?
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Because he's like a real controversial guy. He says some things that are true though.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: For example, he said they're going to fly planes into the Twin Towers.
lines.label: punchline

lines.text: That's not funny. It's just true.
lines.label: tag

lines.text: He said that of June of 2001. He did. Right.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Right. Now I'm not a college-educated individual.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: But I do know that June comes before September
lines.label: setup

lines.text: Like I said, I didn't go to college.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: I dropped out of high school, so I don't know
lines.label: setup

lines.text: if they're putting chemicals in the water
lines.label: setup

lines.text: that are turned the frickin' frogs gay.
lines.label: setup

lines.text: But I do know that the frogs probably didn't want to be outed.
